items.headline: Chris Sale works 6 shutout innings for 150th career win as Braves beat Phillies 6-2
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — Chris Sale shut down the Phillies for the second time in nine days and won his 150th career game as the Atlanta Braves built an early lead and beat Philadelphia 6-2 on Sunday.
Sale (5-1) threw six shutout innings, allowing just one hit and striking out a season-high nine. In his previous start on against the slumping Phillies on April 18, Sale gave up one run in seven innings.

items.headline: Contreras homers, Red Sox respond to Cora firing with 5-3 win over Orioles
items.description:
BALTIMORE (AP) — Willson Contreras hit his sixth home run of the season and second in as many games, and the Boston Red Sox responded to the firing of manager Alex Cora with a 5-3 victory over the Baltimore Orioles on Sunday.
Connelly Early (2-1) allowed two runs in 6 2/3 innings in the rubber game victory for Boston, less than 24 hours after the club parted with Cora and five assistants following a 10-17 start to the season.

items.headline: Coco Gauff overcomes vomiting on court to beat Sorana Cirstea in Madrid
items.description:
MADRID (AP) — Coco Gauff has advanced to the fourth round of the Madrid Open despite falling ill and vomiting on court.
Gauff, a finalist in Madrid last year, rallied to beat Sorana Cirstea 4-6, 7-5, 6-1 on Sunday.

items.headline: Barnes, Ingram score 23 points apiece as Raptors beat Cavaliers 93-89 and tie series at 2-all
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — Scottie Barnes hit the go-ahead free throws in the final minute and scored 23 points, Brandon Ingram also added 23 points and the Toronto Raptors beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 93-89 on Sunday, tying the Eastern Conference first-round playoff series at two games apiece.
The best-of-seven series shifts to Cleveland for Game 5 on Wednesday.

items.headline: Direction of the Boston Red Sox feels 'up in the air' after Cora's firing, Trevor Story says
items.description:
BALTIMORE (AP) — Less than 24 hours after the Boston Red Sox shockingly fired manager Alex Cora and five members of his coaching staff, infielder Trevor Story said the club’s path forward feels unclear.
“I mean obviously, it’s kind of up in the air what the true direction is,” the two-time All-Star said on Sunday morning before a series finale against the Baltimore Orioles. “Those are conversations that need to be had. They’ll be had today and onward going forward, too.”

items.headline: Rinku stars as Kolkata edge Lucknow in Super Over
items.description: Rinku Singh hit an unbeaten 83 and took five catches as Kolkata Knight Riders edged out Lucknow Super Giants in a Super Over...

items.headline: Spurs says star Victor Wembanyama is available for Game 4 against Trail Blazers after clearing NBA's concussion protocol
items.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Spurs says star Victor Wembanyama is available for Game 4 against Trail Blazers after clearing NBA's concussion protocol.

items.headline: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama starts Game 4 vs. Trail Blazers after clearing concussion protocol
items.description:
PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama started Sunday in Game 4 of the team's first-round playoff series against the Portland Trail Blazers after clearing the NBA's concussion protocol.
Wembanyama, the league’s first unanimous Defensive Player of the Year, missed Game 3 on Friday in Portland. The Spurs won 120-108 to go up 2-1 in the series.

items.headline: Sevilla suffer late blow, stay in Liga drop zone
items.description: Sevilla plunged deeper into danger of relegation from La Liga with a 2-1 defeat at Osasuna on Sunday, while Elche picked up three vital points with...

items.headline: Crosby, Penguins aim to extend season again vs. Flyers. Mammoth look for 3-1 lead on Golden Knights
items.description:
At one point, Sidney Crosby could have seen more of himself on an interstate billboard than on a playoff highlight reel for the Penguins.
Ask anyone who has followed his career, the two-time NHL MVP doesn’t stay down for long.
